title: eriodendron grandiflorum
intro: Eriodendron grandiflorum, commonly known as the Brazil nut tree or Pará nut tree, is a species of tree in the family Sapindaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest in South America. The tree's seeds, commonly known as Brazil nuts, have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Brazil nuts include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system health. The seeds can be prepared for consumption through various herbal preparations, such as crushing or grinding them into a powder, or using them as a supplement in capsules or tablets.
